answer:
\boxed{C}

Convolutional networks (CNNs) are the most effective architecture for classifying high-resolution images due to their ability to capture spatial hierarchies and local patterns efficiently. Options like graph networks (A) or RNNs (I) are not designed for image data, while fully connected networks (D) struggle with high-dimensional inputs. Autoencoders (B) and SVMs (F) are not primarily classification architectures for this task.
